prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work or vacation days. Use this for "I want to take vacation" or "request time off"
- travel_suggestion: asking for travel ideas, destinations, or recommendations (not requesting time off)
- payday: asking when salary/paycheck arrives or is scheduled
- direct_deposit: setting up, changing, or inquiring about direct deposit payment method for receiving salary
- book_hotel: booking or finding a hotel to stay at (focus on lodging need)
- restaurant_reviews: specifically seeking restaurant opinions/ratings (focus on dining reviews)
- pay_bill: paying bills owed to others (utilities, credit cards, etc.)

When text mentions both salary and deposit/payment method, choose direct_deposit if about payment setup/process, or payday if about timing of receiving money.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work or vacation days. Use this for "I want to take vacation" or "request time off"
- travel_suggestion: asking for travel ideas, destinations, or recommendations (not requesting time off)
- payday: asking when salary/paycheck arrives or is scheduled
- direct_deposit: setting up, changing, or inquiring about direct deposit payment method for receiving salary. This includes services or methods for paying employees their salaries (e.g., payroll systems, employee payment options)
- pay_bill: paying bills owed to others (utilities, credit cards, invoices), NOT paying employees or setting up employee payroll
- book_hotel: booking or finding a hotel to stay at (focus on lodging need)
- restaurant_reviews: specifically seeking restaurant opinions/ratings (focus on dining reviews)

When text mentions both salary and deposit/payment method, choose direct_deposit if about payment setup/process, or payday if about timing of receiving money.

When text is about paying employees or employee payment methods (payroll), use direct_deposit, not pay_bill.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
